summary: Production Scale Readiness Phase A/B/C fully tested. All 25 backend API tests passed (100%). System health observability endpoints work correctly with admin-only access. Worker queue architecture has 8 queue types (text, image, video, audio, export, webhook, analytics, batch) with per-queue concurrency limits, timeouts, retry configs, per-user fairness (MAX_JOBS_PER_USER=3), dead-letter queue, cancellation support, and p95/p99 metrics tracking. Database indexes exist for funnel_events, asset_access_log, abuse_events collections. k6 load test infrastructure is in place at /app/load-tests/ with mixed-workload.js, scenarios.json (smoke/ramp/spike/soak profiles), and RUNBOOK.md. Previous funnel tracking and streaks APIs still work (regression passed).
critical_code_review_comments: worker_queues.py: Well-structured with 8 QueueType enum values (TEXT, IMAGE, VIDEO, AUDIO, EXPORT, WEBHOOK, ANALYTICS, BATCH). QUEUE_CONFIG has per-queue max_concurrent (1-5), timeout_seconds (30-1800), retry_limit (1-5), retry_delays arrays.
worker_queues.py: WorkerQueue class has cancel_job() method that cancels active tasks and updates DB status to CANCELLED. Tracks _active_user_map for per-user fairness with MAX_JOBS_PER_USER=3.

worker_queues.py: Metrics include processed, succeeded, failed, retried, dead_lettered, cancelled, avg_processing_time, p95_processing_time, p99_processing_time. Processing times stored in rolling 100-sample window.
system_health_api.py: /overview returns comprehensive health data: queues, workers, completion_times, errors, dead_letter, database, request_latency (p50/p95/p99), asset_access, uptime_seconds.
system_health_api.py: /queues returns per-queue detail for all 8 queue types with queued/processing/max_wait_seconds/failed_last_hour counts.
system_health_api.py: /dead-letter and /stuck-jobs endpoints provide admin visibility into failed and stuck jobs.
system_health_api.py: All endpoints use get_admin_user dependency for admin-only access (returns 403 for non-admin).
database_indexes.py: INDEX_DEFINITIONS includes funnel_events (4 indexes), asset_access_log (4 indexes), abuse_events (3 indexes) with proper compound indexes for query performance.
server.py: LatencyTrackingMiddleware records request latencies to in-memory tracker, feeding p50/p95/p99 metrics to system_health_api.

k6 load tests: mixed-workload.js has traffic distribution (40% landing, 15% auth, 15% dashboard, 10% generation, 5% pricing, 5% share, 5% asset, 5% admin). scenarios.json has smoke_100, ramp_500/1k/3k/5k/10k, spike, soak_1h profiles.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Production Scale Readiness infrastructure is fully operational. System health observability at /api/admin/system-health/* provides real-time visibility into queue depth, worker status, completion times, error rates, dead letter queue, DB health, and request latency percentiles. Worker queue architecture supports 8 queue types with per-user fairness (MAX_JOBS_PER_USER=3), job cancellation, dead-letter handling, and p95/p99 metrics. k6 load test suite at /app/load-tests/ is ready for production testing but cannot be executed at full scale in preview environment. Database indexes for funnel_events, asset_access_log, abuse_events are defined and created at startup.
